revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14858 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/d6a6808c-8c94-419b-8468-386336ff0f85 Kurume Bureau of Tourism and International Exchange] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14858 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/d6a6808c-8c94-419b-8468-386336ff0f85 Kurume Bureau of Tourism and International Exchange] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | Group = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14858 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/d6a6808c-8c94-419b-8468-386336ff0f85 Kurume Bureau of Tourism and International Exchange] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=draft}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14858 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/d6a6808c-8c94-419b-8468-386336ff0f85 Kurume Bureau of Tourism and International Exchange] {{in lang|ja}} Urayama Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14858 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/d6a6808c-8c94-419b-8468-386336ff0f85 Kurume Bureau of Tourism and International Exchange] {{in lang|ja}} Urayama Kofun

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Kurume, Fukuoka]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[Kurume, Fukuoka|Kurume]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14858 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/d6a6808c-8c94-419b-8468-386336ff0f85 Kurume Bureau of Tourism and International Exchange] {{in lang|ja}} Urayama Kofun

The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[:en:Nichirinji Kofun|Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[:en:Naritasan Kurume Bunin|Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Kurume Station|Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Urayama Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[:en:Nichirinji Kofun|Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[:en:Naritasan Kurume Bunin|Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Kurume Station|Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Urayama Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[:en:Nichirinji Kofun|Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[:en:Naritasan Kurume Bunin|Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Kurume Station|Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Urayama Kofun]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Urayama Kofun | native_name =浦山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Urayama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E|region:JP-40|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|17|06|N|130|32|09|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[:en:Nichirinji Kofun|Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[:en:Naritasan Kurume Bunin|Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Kurume Station|Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14858 Fukuoka Tourism Web] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://welcome-kurume.com/spots/detail/d6a6808c-8c94-419b-8468-386336ff0f85 Kurume Bureau of Tourism and International Exchange] {{in lang|ja}} Urayama Kofun

The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[:en:Naritasan Kurume Bunin|Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Kurume Station|Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].

The {{nihongo|'''Urayama Kofun'''|浦山古墳}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamizu neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1951.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173245|title=浦山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Urayama Kofun is located on a small hill commonly called Mount Chabozu, which is derived from Mt. Myojo and Mt. Tobidake at the western end of the Minou Mountains that limit the southern part of the Chikugo Plain. This {{nihongo|scallop-shaped kofun|帆立型古墳|hotate-gata kofun}} is 60 meters long and was constructed in part by carving out a portion of the natural hill. Inside the posterior circular portion is a horizontal entry-style stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] orientated to the northwest. It contains large house-shaped sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]]. The lid is in the shape of a four-pillar roof, with two ring-shaped hanging protrusions on each side, and the body consists of four walls and a bottom made of one stone each. Decorative patterns are engraved on the inside of the sarcophagus, and the upper and lower tiers of the side and back walls of the burial chamber are decorated with straight arc pattern belts, with the middle tier decorated with double circle pattern belts. Traces of vermilion paint still remain in some parts. Simple straight-arch patterns are also inscribed on the inside and outside surfaces of the front wall. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include ''[[magatama]]'' beads, gold rings, iron swords, and armor. From these artifacts, it is believed that the tomb is slightly older than the similarly decorated [[:en:Nichirinji Kofun|Nichirinji Kofun]] and was built in the latter half of the 5th century.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The burial chamber is now protected by a covered roof, permitting visits to see the sarcophagus and a portion of the wall engravings. The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[:en:Naritasan Kurume Bunin|Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Kurume Station|Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].

The tumulus is located within the grounds of the [[:en:Naritasan Kurume Bunin|Naritasan Kurume Bunin]] temple, approximately 6.1 kilometers southeast of [[:en:Kurume Station|Kurume Station]] or 5.3 kilometers east of [[:en:Araki Station (Fukuoka)|Araki Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Kagoshima Main Line|Kagoshima Main Line]].
